meaning: vt.鏀惧皠,杈愬皠,浼犳挱,骞挎挱vi.鍙戝厜,杈愬皠,娴侀湶adj.鏈夊皠绾跨殑,杈愬皠鐘剁殑
examples: Five roads radiate from the square.
Callahan posits that chemical elements radiate electromagnetic signals.
Many kinds of woodland can be seen on the various walks which radiate from the Heritage Centre.
As Parisians settle down to sleep, trains radiate out across the continent in all directions.
Like an earthquake, the shock waves radiate from Terry and the aftershocks keep on coming.
